questions.question: Which CSS declaration creates a flex container?
questions.explanation: display: flex on a container element establishes a flex formatting context, making its direct children flex items.

questions.question: What does `justify-content: space-between` do?
choices: Centers all items in the container
Puts equal space between items, with no space at the edges
Puts equal space around each item, including at the edges
Aligns items to the start of the container
questions.explanation: space-between distributes items so the first is at the start, the last is at the end, and remaining space is evenly divided between items.

questions.question: Which property controls the direction of the main axis in a flex container?
questions.explanation: flex-direction sets the main axis direction: row (horizontal) or column (vertical), plus their reverse variants.

questions.question: What does `align-items: center` do in a flex container with the default flex-direction?
choices: Centers items horizontally along the main axis
Centers items vertically along the cross axis
Centers the entire flex container on the page
Centers text inside each flex item
questions.explanation: With the default flex-direction: row, align-items operates on the cross (vertical) axis, centering items vertically within the container.

questions.question: The ___ property allows flex items to wrap onto multiple lines when they exceed the container width.
questions.explanation: flex-wrap: wrap allows items to flow onto new lines instead of being squeezed into a single line.

questions.question: The ___ property determines how much a flex item grows relative to other items when extra space is available.
questions.explanation: flex-grow accepts a unitless number that dictates what proportion of available space a flex item should take up.
